A.evidence: Main prose is mostly present, but second author is misplaced after the footnote/intro, algorithm math has OCR tokens like /epsilon1 and /circledot, and many displayed equations are replaced by formula-not-decoded.

B.evidence: Prose and reading order are strong and Algorithm 1 is mostly usable, but some math is wrong such as sqrt(v_t) instead of sqrt(hat v_t), eq. 4 drops beta_2^t, and Theorem 4.1 uses alpha/sqrt(T).

C.evidence: Early prose is usable, but Algorithm 1 is truncated/mangled after m_0, section 2.1 loses much of the paragraph before section 3, and Theorem 4.1 formula is badly corrupted.

D.evidence: Best overall: prose, algorithms, equations, and section order are mostly faithful, with minor issues like a literal \nend while in Algorithm 1 and a small symbol error in the cancellation equation.
